opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My doctor's message said my iron is extremely low and I have an autoimmune disease. My heart is racing, I can't catch my breath, and I feel like I'm going to pass out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I just read my blood test results online and I'm terrified something is seriously wrong with my heart.

symptom_narrative.onset: about an hour ago, right after I read my blood results on the online portal
symptom_narrative.duration: around an hour and it's still going
symptom_narrative.character: my chest feels tight, like I can't get a full breath, and my heart is pounding
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it feels really severe, like something terrible is about to happen to me
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it came on suddenly and has been pretty constant since
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much — breathing slowly helps a tiny bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about the results makes it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: racing heart, feeling like I might faint, and a bit of tingling in my hands
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've felt anxious before but never anything this intense
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't concentrate on anything and I'm scared to just sit alone with it

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: recently my doctor told me a blood test showed my iron is low and that I might have an autoimmune condition
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: found out on a routine blood test very recently

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone and has been very anxious about health since reading the results without anyone to talk it through with

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, frightened messages fixating on the blood results and on the fear of a heart problem
